results.title: Top 6 Setapp alternatives for downloading macOS and iOS apps
results.content:
Parallels is a Setapp alternative for those who want to run Windows apps on a Mac, while Brewer X and Applite are suitable for tech-savvy users who are familiar with Homebrew. On MacUpdate, you can find plenty of apps and reviews for them, while Softorino has a narrow collection of tools for everyday tasks.

Don't want to spend time and money testing different Mac apps and stressing about their security? Try Setapp Membership and get a handpicked, secure collection of tools at a single subscription price. Or use Setapp Marketplace to get the exact app you need, with the same Setapp quality and safety standards.

## FAQ

### What alternatives to Setapp are available? [...] ## FAQ

### What alternatives to Setapp are available?

There are several Setapp alternatives for Mac and iOS. Depending on what apps you're looking for, you can explore Softorino, MacUpdate, Applite, and other platforms. Make sure to research the details of each before installing.

### Are Setapp alternatives safe?

Each platform provides different security measures, including developer verification and malware testing. Check the security guarantees on the specific Setapp alternative platform site before downloading apps.

### What are some free Setapp alternatives?

Platforms that provide free access to Mac apps include Applite, Homebrew, and MacUpdate (with some paid features). You can also try Setapp for free within a 7-day trial.

### Can Setapp be trusted? [...] Brewer X

### Applite: Visual interface for Homebrew apps

As a free Setapp alternative for Mac, Applite provides access to an expanded collection of popular apps like Google Chrome, Notion, Spotify and many others. Applite package manager is powered by Homebrew. Users can download, update, and uninstall Mac apps with a single click on this open-source platform.

Pros:

Cons:

Apps included:

Applite

Applite

## How to choose Setapp alternative: My takeaway

The only way to discover which Setapp alternative is right for you is to dive deep into each of the app platforms.

Despite the Mac App Store's wide variety of apps, there is a risk of getting overwhelmed by the choices and not finding exactly what you need.

results.title: I Replaced these four Setapp apps on my Mac with free alternatives
results.content:
1.   Replaced Bartender with Ice: Bartender is great for managing menu bar icons, but I found Ice to be a solid open-source alternative. It’s free and does the job just as well.
2.   Switched CleanMyMac for Onyx: CleanMyMac is a powerful tool for cleaning up your Mac, but Onyx offers similar functionality. There is no nice UI, but it gets the job done when we talk about mac maintenance. It has not the security analysis, nor the uninstaller you can find in CleanMyMac, but it’s a great tool.

I’ve been a Setapp user for a few years now, I started my subscription just after the beginning of this service. Setapp offers a fantastic catalog of apps for a monthly fee, t’s like the “Netflix” for Mac apps. It’s been a convenient way to access a variety of tools without having to purchase each one individually. However, I’ve recently come experiencing «subscription fatigue.»

## The subscription fatigue

Subscription fatigue is that feeling you get when you’re paying for more services than you actually use or need. I realized that I was paying for a bunch of apps that I didn’t really need.

## Making the switch

So, I decided to make some changes and replaced the apps I use the most by free alternatives: [...] Sign up with Facebook

results.title: Setapp: App Store alternative offers 200+ Mac apps at an attractive price | Macworld
results.content:
The Setapp subscription bundle offers an intriguing combination of the above. Run as a division of software developer MacPaw, it’s a third-party monthly or annual license to over 200 Mac apps that seems to offer the best of all worlds for app users and software developers alike. The collection of apps provides unlimited, ad-free access to a subscriber for as long as they’re paying the relatively modest recurring fee.

The real question with Setapp is whether the included apps that you choose to use total more than the $9.99-per-month ($107.88-per-year) subscription price. You should also factor in the utility of having so many apps at your fingertips while never having to manage or pay for upgrades with an active Setapp subscription. [...] You can subscribe to Setapp and proceed to install and use all of those macOS apps above and every one of the 200-odd others without paying an extra cent above the subscription price. This includes Bartender (menu-bar organization), Default Folder (improved file dialog management), Luminar (AI-powered photo editing), Hype (animated web content creator), Disk Drill (data recovery), NetSpotand WiFi Explorer (wireless network planning, deployment, and monitoring), and MarsEdit (blog-post writing software that works with most blog platforms). Many Setapp programs have received high marks in Macworld reviews or were recommended in articles like those linked in this paragraph. [...] When you purchase through links in our articles, we may earn a small commission. This doesn't affect our editorial independence.

Feature

# Setapp: App Store alternative offers 200+ Mac apps at an attractive price

Setapp bundles over 200 Mac apps for a flat price that adds up to a bargain for many users.

Glenn Fleishman

By Glenn Fleishman

Contributor, Macworld   JAN 4, 2022 3:00 am PST

Setapp

Image: MacPaw

results.title: Leaving Setapp — 23 Alternatives & 3 Holdouts
results.content:
brew install --cask middle
Rectangle is a powerful and free window-snapping utility with all the expected options. Many similar utilities exist, but Rectangle comes highly recommended, and so far, I’m happy with it.

brew install --cask rectangle
## CleanMyMac = Mole&Pear Cleaner — Free (open source)

CleanMyMac has long been one of the first apps I install on a new machine. The problem is, without Setapp, CleanMyMac would cost $16 a month or nearly $200 one-time (for the Pro version with all the features). This app alone kept me on Setapp long after I’d started to doubt its continued value. I was ready to give up again when I found Mole. [...] brew install --cask keka
## Bartender = iBar Pro — $10

Even before deciding to cancel Setapp, I was looking for a replacement for Bartender. Beyond some valid privacy concerns with its new owners, updates have stalled. It still isn’t entirely compatible with Tahoe; when it wasn’t crashing, icons would shift around randomly and sometimes just disappear.

The standard recommendations — Ice, Vanilla, Dozer, and Hidden Bar, specifically — work well, but are missing one vital feature: They can’t display hidden icons on a floating bar below the standard menu bar. Well, Ice has that feature, but it crashed every time I tried to enable it. If you don’t need that feature, all four are worth checking out. [...] # Leaving Setapp — 23 Alternatives & 3 Holdouts

Image 2: Jeremiah_Clark

Jeremiah_Clark

Follow

9 min read

·

Apr 21, 2026

.

## Worth Simply Buying

To kick things off, three of the Setapp apps I used regularly were just so good, or so one-of-a-kind, that I went ahead and bought them.

### Antinote — $5

Fantastic little notepad that lives in your menu bar. As a bonus, it handles basic math — like keeping running totals — so it replaced Numi for me as well.

brew install --cask antinote
### Popclip — $12

There is nothing else quite like PopClip. The price is a steal for how much genuine utility I get from it.

brew install --cask popclip
### Typeface — $43

results.title: Replacements for some Setapp apps - Tech Support - MPU Talk
results.content:
For Downie, there is a Raycast extension to download YouTube videos.

Bloom is an excellent finder alternative instead of Forklift.

For CleanmyMacX, there is Onyx which is a free alternative that’s been around for awhile.

Finally checkout Shottr for a CleanshotX alternative.

First, IT blacklisted any mail clients besides a few select ones like Outlook and Mail.app; next to go was all but a few calendar apps — again Outlook, and Calendar.app.

I think the main concern is if any company pulls any info over to its own servers (rather than doing everything on-device). But MS has a BAA agreement with my work, and I think Apple was included upon review of iCloud encryption settings. [...] BusyCal, Forklift and CleanShot X all seem to have the same licensing scheme. You can buy a certain period (often a year) of updates and keep using the last of those updates for free after that (until the OS or something else breaks functionality; you can then decide to buy another set of updates).

PS: Just stumbled upon this website which might help in finding alternatives for subscription apps:

### PayOnceApps.com - One-Time Purchase Web, Windows & Mac apps

Explore PayOnceApps.com for top-quality web, Windows, and Mac applications at a one-time purchase price. Save big on premium software without recurring fees. Perpetual licenses, one-time purchase

OpenIn [...] Regular: Forecast Bar, Mission Control Plus, CleanMyMac, PopClip, Pareto Security, ForkLift, iStat Menus, CleanShot X, Default Folder X, Time Out, Nitro PDF Pro, HoudahSpot. Will use Bartender again if it ever gets fixed. Was one of my favorite apps before it fell on hard times.

Occasional: Downie, Archiver

Every great once in a while: KeyCue, BetterZip, Novabench, SnapMotion, DisplayBuddy, PixelSnap, TextSniper, PDF Squeezer.

I’ll likely try Moonlock again after it has time to smooth out.

So I think that I’m getting my money’s worth. If NitroPDF Pro ever gets to the point where it can completely replace Adobe Acrobat, I’ll then be saving tons of money.

And I like trying out new stuff that appears on Setapp once in a while.
